items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent` **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` **Triggered by:** fea03a2e83e62e5e85d1d4b1f65c946c976fb5e4 (#58169) by `@live1206` ## What Changed PR #58169 separated the previously unified `sdk-migration` skill into two distinct skills: - `.github/skills/dpg-migration/SKILL.md` ‚Äî data-plane migration workflow - `.github/skills/mpg-migration/SKILL.md` ‚Äî management-plane migration workflow (newly added) The `README.md` was updated to reference both skills throughout, but the **Examples** section's "Use with Copilot Chat" subsection was not updated to include an MPG migration example. ## Gaps Found - The **`Examples ‚Üí Use with Copilot Chat`** section only shows how to invoke `dpg-migration` via Copilot Chat prompt, with no equivalent example for `mpg-migration` - The MPG migration skill has different required inputs (targets `Azure.ResourceManager.*` packages; spec repo path is optional since it defaults to `../azure-rest-api-specs`) and the prompt pattern should be documented in parallel with the DPG example - The "Use with a migration skill" subsection describes a generic workflow that pre-dates the split; it could call out the setup-phase difference between DPG and MPG (MPG uses `RegenSdkLocal.ps1` rather than `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`)
üìê Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` ‚Äî **edit** In the `## Examples` section, the `### Use with Copilot Chat` subsection currently ends after the DPG example. Add an MPG migration subsection immediately after the closing paragraph of the DPG section: ````markdown For management-plane libraries (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`), use the `mpg-migration` skill: ``` Use the mpg-migration skill to migrate (repo-root)/sdk/(service)/Azure.ResourceManager.(Service) The local specs repo is at (specs-root)/specification/(service)/(spec-directory) ``` For example: ``` Use the mpg-migration skill to migrate C:\git\azure-sdk-for-net\sdk\compute\Azure.ResourceManager.Compute The local specs repo is at C:\git\azure-rest-api-specs\specification\compute\resource-manager ``` If the specs repo is already at the default sibling path (`../azure-rest-api-specs`), you can omit the path: ``` Use the mpg-migration skill to migrate C:\git\azure-sdk-for-net\sdk\compute\Azure.ResourceManager.Compute ``` ```` Also update the `### Use with a migration skill` subsection to briefly note the setup-phase difference between DPG and MPG. Replace the current generic workflow list: ```markdown The tools are designed to be called by the dedicated migration skills. Use [`dpg-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/dpg-migration/SKILL.md) for data-plane libraries and [`mpg-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mpg-migration/SKILL.md) for management-plane libraries. The workflows share the same build-fix loop but differ in setup: **DPG (data-plane) workflow:** 1. **Setup** ‚Äî `pregen_cleanup` ‚Üí `validate_tsp_config` ‚Üí `commit_iteration` ‚Üí `run_code_generation` 2. **Build-fix loop** ‚Äî `build_and_classify` ‚Üí `batch_fix` (for deterministic errors) ‚Üí LLM reasoning (for non-deterministic errors) ‚Üí rebuild 3. **Finalize** ‚Äî `migrate_test_samples` ‚Üí `run_tests` ‚Üí `finalize_migration` **MPG (management-plane) workflow:** 1. **Setup** ‚Äî Update `tsp-location.yaml` ‚Üí `run_code_generation` (or `RegenSdkLocal.ps1`) 2. **Build-fix loop** ‚Äî `build_and_classify` ‚Üí `batch_fix` (deterministic) ‚Üí spec-side decorator fixes ‚Üí rebuild 3. **Breaking-change mitigation** ‚Äî Export API ‚Üí `batch_fix` ‚Üí re-export ‚Üí validate 4. **Finalize** ‚Äî `finalize_migration` ‚Üí `run_tests` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet changes required ‚Äî this is pure prose documentation. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` already contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ‚úÖ Getting started (Install/Build, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ‚úÖ Key concepts 3. ‚öÝÔ∏è **Examples** ‚Äî "Use with Copilot Chat" subsection is missing MPG example (this issue) 4. ‚úÖ Troubleshooting 5. ‚úÖ Next steps 6. ‚úÖ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tools` ‚Äî confirm no unexpected README changes 4. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [GeneratorAgent] Docs: Add MPG migration Copilot Chat example to README
